summary:
Verubecestat has not demonstrated efficacy in Alzheimer’s disease due to lack of data from pivotal clinical trials. It was neither studied extensively nor approved by regulators despite targeting amyloid production through gamma-secretase inhibition. 

The summary above contains irrelevant content about related drugs like Aducanumab—please revise accordingly.

Correct Summary:

Verubecestat has not shown efficacy in Alzheimer’s disease according to available clinical evidence. Due to the complete absence of reported trial results demonstrating cognitive improvement or significant reduction in amyloid burden, its use for treating AD remains unsupported.
